data.content:
B客戶
B客戶 is a term used to denote a customer in the context of maintenance services. This entity represents the individual or organization that receives various forms of maintenance support. B客戶 is identified as the recipient of maintenance services, encompassing both current and past service provisions. The term is used to describe a customer for whom maintenance services are provided, are being provided, were provided, or will be performed. This indicates that B客戶 can be associated with maintenance activities at different stages—whether ongoing, completed, or yet to commence.

B客戶 is linked to specific maintenance records, suggesting a formal documentation process that tracks the services rendered to this customer. The term emphasizes the importance of maintaining detailed records for each customer to ensure efficient and effective service delivery. This association with maintenance records underscores the systematic approach taken in managing and documenting the services provided to B客戶.

The descriptions highlight several key aspects of B客戶's role, reinforcing that B客戶 is the customer for whom maintenance services were provided, are being provided, or will be performed. The consistent use of the term across different contexts underscores its significance in the maintenance service industry, where accurate record-keeping and timely service delivery are crucial.<SEP>B客戶is the customer for whom maintenance services are being performed.

A人員
A人員 is an engineer primarily focused on performing a variety of maintenance tasks. This role encompasses confirming account permissions, managing service transfers, and conducting maintenance services, with a particular focus on activities related to Splunk. A人員's duties are crucial for ensuring the smooth operation and upkeep of systems, making them integral to the maintenance process.

A人員's involvement in maintenance is not limited to a single client; they are also involved in performing maintenance tasks for B客戶. This highlights their versatility and importance across different clients and systems. The consistent mention of A人員 as engineers underscores their technical expertise and the critical nature of their role in maintaining operational efficiency.

A人員's responsibilities include working on DEP02, backup scripts, PA reports, and system upgrades. These activities collectively emphasize that A人員 are essential for the upkeep and smooth functioning of various systems. Their range of maintenance activities contributes to the overall integrity and performance of the systems they support. Additionally, A人員 is involved in IP adjustments and software upgrades, further demonstrating their broad skill set and importance in maintaining system functionality.

Splunk_11月維護
Splunk_11月維護refers to the November maintenance event for Splunk, involving various actions and recommendations.<SEP>Splunk_11月維護refers to a specific maintenance event for Splunk, focusing on various performance metrics and system evaluations.

620.21GB
620.21GB represents the average authorized usage, indicating higher demand than the current capacity.

PM (定期保養)
PM (定期保養) refers to preventive maintenance activities performed regularly to ensure system reliability and performance.<SEP>PM (定期保養) refers to preventive maintenance, a scheduled type of maintenance aimed at preventing equipment failures and ensuring optimal performance.<SEP>PM (定期保養) refers to the type of maintenance performed, which is periodic maintenance.<SEP>PM (定期保養) refers to a preventive maintenance method involving regular servicing and upkeep.

10.0.2/2025.11.14
10.0.2/2025.11.14 is the latest version of a software system, released on November 14, 2025.<SEP>10.0.2/2025.11.14 is the latest version of the Splunk credit card processing system, recommended for update due to security concerns.<SEP>10.0.2/2025.11.14 is the latest version of Splunk, recommended for update to address security vulnerabilities.

285GB
285GB is the current authorized capacity for a system.<SEP>285GB represents the authorized data usage limit for Splunk's software.<SEP>285GB represents the recommended authorization quantity for Splunk B部門.<SEP>285GB is the recommended authorization quantity for the system.

9.3.6/2025.07.31
9.3.6/2025.07.31 is the current version of a software system, released on July 31, 2025.<SEP>9.3.6/2025.07.31 is the current version of Splunk's software as of July 31, 2025.

World Athletics Federation
The World Athletics Federation, formerly known as the International Association of Athletics Federations (IAAF), is the global governing body for athletics. It oversees a variety of significant international competitions, most notably the World Athletics Championships, which are held biennially and encompass a wide array of track and field events. The federation is also responsible for validating world records across various athletic disciplines, ensuring that performances adhere to the required standards and criteria.

In addition to the World Athletics Championships, the World Athletics Federation manages other major international competitions such as the World Indoor Championships and the World Relays. These events are crucial in promoting athletics on a global scale. The federation plays a pivotal role not only in organizing these competitions but also in setting rules and regulations that govern the sport. It is deeply committed to supporting the development of athletics at all levels, from grassroots initiatives to elite competitions.

The World Athletics Federation's responsibilities extend beyond just overseeing championships and validating records. It is instrumental in promoting the sport worldwide, ensuring that athletics continues to thrive and evolve. By setting standards and providing support, the federation helps maintain the integrity and growth of athletics globally.<SEP>The World Athletics Federation is the governing body overseeing the World Athletics Championship and record validations.

Noah Carter
The World Athletics Federation validates Noah Carter's new 100m sprint record set at the championship.

HTTPS Large Transmission Alert
The HTTPS large transmission alert involves adjusting settings to exclude M365 addresses and creating a new report for daily HTTP data transmission statistics.

Splunk
Splunk is a software company known for its data analytics and monitoring solutions. The company specializes in providing tools designed for searching, monitoring, and analyzing machine-generated data. This capability enables organizations to gain valuable insights from their vast amounts of data, which is crucial for leveraging data effectively.

Splunk's software platform is used extensively for managing and analyzing events, including those related to firewalls. The company offers a range of services that include data monitoring and analysis, which are essential for businesses aiming to make informed decisions based on real-time data insights. This approach helps improve operational efficiency and strategic planning within organizations.

Splunk's commitment to continuous improvement is evident through its periodic maintenance and updates. For instance, the software underwent an upgrade from version 9.3.1 to 9.3.3, highlighting the company's dedication to enhancing its capabilities. Additionally, Splunk performed maintenance on its software in October and December, emphasizing the importance of keeping the system up-to-date with IP adjustments and other necessary upgrades.

Splunk is also involved in maintaining its services and products, which includes tasks such as disabling version checks during upgrades. The company's monitoring system was configured to handle similar log files more effectively, showcasing its focus on security and efficiency. Furthermore, Splunk upgraded its software from version 9.3.3 to 9.3.8 during the maintenance process.

Overall, Splunk provides a comprehensive suite of data analytics and monitoring solutions that help organizations optimize their operations through real-time data insights.<SEP>Splunk is a company whose software requires maintenance and updates.<SEP>Splunk is a company whose services and accounts were managed during the maintenance, including account modifications and historical case reviews with Arcsight.<SEP>Splunk is a software platform used by E客戶, requiring monthly maintenance and updates.<SEP>Splunk is a company that provides software for searching, monitoring, and analyzing machine-generated data.<SEP>Splunk is a company that provides services, including the maintenance described in the record.

GeoDB
GeoDB is a database that was updated as part of the maintenance activities.<SEP>GeoDB is a database that was updated as part of the maintenance activities.<SEP>GeoDB is a database that was updated as part of the weekly work items.<SEP>GeoDB is a database that was updated as part of the maintenance activities.<SEP>GeoDB is a database that was updated as part of the maintenance activities.<SEP>GeoDB is a database that was updated as part of the weekly maintenance tasks.<SEP>GeoDB is a database that was updated as part of the maintenance activities.

Disk Usage
Disk usage refers to the storage capacity consumption of FWD02, which reached 90% and was addressed by cleaning up estreamer data.

M365
M365 is an address that was excluded from the HTTPS large transmission alert, with its own separate report for daily HTTP data transmission.

CPU Usage
CPU usage refers to the processing power consumption of FWD02, which increased significantly starting from 12/9.

FWD02
FWD02 is a system experiencing high CPU and disk usage, which has been addressed by adjusting settings and cleaning up estreamer data.<SEP>FWD02 is a device or system with magnetic disk storage experiencing high usage.

Estreamer Data
Estreamer data is a type of data that occupies significant storage space on FWD02, with its retention period adjusted to reduce disk usage.<SEP>Estreamer data refers to the information processed by estreamer, which has been increasing rapidly.

FCB Script
The FCB script is associated with the addition of audit rules and the transfer of audit logs to /var/log/secure, leading to increased estreamer data.

Audit Logs
Audit logs are records generated by the FCB script that were transferred to /var/log/secure, contributing to increased estreamer data.

SSH Server Vulnerability Patch
The SSH server vulnerability patch is a pending task related to maintaining system security.

A部門
A部門 is a department with a broad range of responsibilities related to maintaining and managing various aspects of Splunk's infrastructure and user permissions. One of its key roles involves adjusting user permissions in Splunk and setting capability values for specific roles, such as maintainer and ess_analyst. This includes handling account inventory verification and reporting tasks, ensuring that all accounts are properly inventoried and reported to relevant parties like C同事.

Additionally, A部門 is tasked with maintaining account permissions and confirming service transfers, which involves verifying the accuracy of user access levels and ensuring smooth transitions when services are moved or reassigned. This department also plays a crucial role in checking report deliveries, making sure that all reports are sent out correctly and on time. For instance, they send reports to Hank as part of their account inventory and reporting tasks.

A部門 is also responsible for handling PA reports and related issues. This includes scheduling these reports and resolving any errors that may arise during the process. They address specific issues such as Splunk role capabilities and resending monthly PA reports when necessary. Their responsibilities extend to various maintenance tasks related to Splunk's infrastructure, ensuring that the system runs smoothly and efficiently.

In summary, A部門 is a multifaceted department that handles a wide array of tasks, from user permissions and account inventory to report management and infrastructure maintenance, all aimed at ensuring the smooth operation of Splunk and its associated services.<SEP>A部門is a department responsible for various Splunk-related tasks, including IP adjustments and software upgrades.

B部門
B部門 is a specialized department responsible for a wide array of technical tasks. One of its primary responsibilities involves handling FISAC (Financial Information System for Accounting and Control) data, which includes accommodation, retrieval, and overall management. This encompasses ensuring the smooth operation of data systems and addressing any anomalies that may arise.

In addition to data management, B部門 is heavily involved in testing the SH KVstore engine. This includes identifying and resolving anomalies within the engine, as well as setting up LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) configurations to ensure secure and efficient data access. The department also manages various maintenance activities related to these systems.

B部門 plays a crucial role in indexer management, particularly with the IDX cluster. This involves adding indexers to the cluster and monitoring their synchronization status to maintain optimal performance. The department is also responsible for tasks such as Centos OS upgrades and IP transfers, which are essential for maintaining the infrastructure's stability and security.

Furthermore, B部門 handles troubleshooting issues related to n8n and Splunk connectivity. This ensures that data flows seamlessly between different systems, enhancing overall operational efficiency. The department's responsibilities also include managing various other maintenance activities, ensuring that all technical aspects of their operations run smoothly.

Fisac Data Accommodation
FISAC data accommodation involves submitting an API IP application form to FISAC and is currently in progress.

Splunk每週onsite
Splunk每週onsite is a weekly onsite maintenance event specifically designed for Splunk. This event involves various tasks and checks to ensure the smooth operation of Splunk systems. Conducted by engineers, including A人員and C人員, the maintenance event encompasses specific tasks and actions that are crucial for upkeeping the system's functionality.

The event is characterized as a method involving on-site work, emphasizing the hands-on nature of the maintenance activities. This weekly routine is essential for maintaining the integrity and performance of Splunk systems, ensuring they remain reliable and efficient.<SEP>Splunk每週onsite is a weekly on-site maintenance event involving various tasks and actions.<SEP>Splunk每週onsite is a weekly onsite maintenance event for Splunk.<SEP>Splunk每週onsite is a weekly on-site maintenance event for Splunk.

Sh Kvstore Engine Anomaly Testing
SH KVstore engine anomaly testing is being conducted in a personal test environment and is ongoing.

Ess_analyst Role
The Ess_analyst role's capability values were adjusted to facilitate analysis by Hank.

Maintainer Role
The Maintainer role is a user permission level in Splunk that was newly created with specific capability values.

Centos Os Upgrade
The Centos OS upgrade is currently under evaluation.

Dep02 Ip Transfer
DEP02 IP transfer is scheduled to be conducted next week.

Linux Host User Permissions
Managing Linux host user permissions and roles is part of the ongoing tasks.

A客戶
A客戶is the customer for whom maintenance services are being performed.<SEP>A客戶is the customer who requested or received the maintenance service.<SEP>A客戶is the customer for whom the maintenance service was provided.<SEP>A客戶is the customer for whom the maintenance was performed.<SEP>A客戶is the customer associated with the maintenance record.

Switch_6FDC.csv
Switch_6FDC.csv is a data file where new equipment was added during the maintenance process.

C人員
C人員 are engineers who play a crucial role in various maintenance activities. They are primarily responsible for performing maintenance tasks, with a specific focus on supporting E客戶 and B客戶. Their duties include executing general maintenance tasks as well as more specialized tasks related to maintaining systems efficiently.

C人員 are involved in managing the Splunk Indexer queue, highlighting their role in both routine and technical aspects of maintenance. They assist with various tasks including KV store certificate updates and Splunk bundle storage issues. While some descriptions mention C人員 directly performing maintenance tasks, others note that they are engineers involved in maintenance services without specifying direct involvement in particular actions. This suggests that C人員 may have varying levels of engagement in different maintenance activities, depending on the context.

Additionally, there is a mention of C人員 being an engineer involved in the maintenance record but not directly involved in the described actions, which could imply administrative or supervisory roles within the maintenance team. Overall, C人員 are integral to ensuring the smooth operation and upkeep of systems through their expertise in maintenance services. They also receive reports from A部門, indicating a collaborative role within the organization.

The descriptions consistently emphasize that C人員 are engineers who perform maintenance tasks, particularly on E客戶's Splunk system. This reinforces their technical expertise and direct involvement in maintaining critical systems. Their roles encompass both hands-on maintenance activities and potentially administrative or supervisory functions, making them essential for the overall efficiency and reliability of the systems they support.<SEP>C人員is another engineer involved in the maintenance service.

Maintenance Record
The Maintenance Record documents the details of a specific maintenance service performed on December 19, 2025.<SEP>The Maintenance Record documents the details of a service event, including customer information and actions taken.<SEP>The Maintenance Record documents the details of a service event for Splunk SIEM, including metadata and target details.<SEP>The Maintenance Record documents the details and actions taken during a maintenance session for Splunk B部門.

ARP_table Report
The ARP_table report is a document that needs to be rerun and scheduled for monthly distribution.

E客戶
E客戶 is a customer who interacts with maintenance services in various capacities. E客戶 requests maintenance services and is also the recipient of these services, indicating that they both initiate the need for maintenance and receive the benefits of the services provided.

The descriptions highlight that E客戶 can be involved in maintenance services at different stages: before, during, and after the service provision. Specifically, E客戶 is a customer who has requested maintenance services and is also the customer for whom these services are being performed or have been completed. This suggests a continuous relationship with maintenance providers, from the initial request to the completion of the work.

E客戶 has specific scheduled maintenance work planned for November 14, 2025. This detail underscores the forward-looking aspect of E客戶's role, indicating that maintenance services are not just reactive but also proactive, with future appointments already in place. Additionally, E客戶 requires assistance with various tasks and has maintenance work scheduled, which may include specific needs such as maintenance for their Splunk deployment.<SEP>E客戶is the customer for whom maintenance services are being provided.<SEP>E客戶is a customer who has maintenance work scheduled.<SEP>E客戶is the customer for whom the maintenance service was performed.

Mitreattack
Mitreattack refers to different attack scenarios for which new alerts were added.

SASE VPN
SASE VPN is a system that requires modifications to its login success report conditions.<SEP>SASE VPN is a virtual private network system that requires modifications to its login success report conditions.

F客戶
F客戶is the customer for whom maintenance services are being performed.<SEP>F客戶is the customer for whom maintenance services are being provided.<SEP>F客戶is the customer for whom maintenance services were provided.<SEP>F客戶is the customer for whom maintenance services are being performed.<SEP>F客戶is the customer for whom maintenance services are being performed.

Splunk Credit Card Processing System
The Splunk credit card processing system underwent December maintenance, with specific actions taken to address performance and security issues.<SEP>The Splunk Credit Card Processing System is the target of the November maintenance activities, involving authorization usage and server performance checks.<SEP>The Splunk credit card processing system is undergoing November maintenance.

137 UF Devices
137 UF devices are identified as having connectivity issues with the indexer, exceeding 15 minutes without connection.

100GB
100GB is the recommended authorization quantity for the Splunk credit card processing system.<SEP>100GB represents the current authorized data usage limit for the system.

124GB
124GB is the average authorized usage of the Splunk credit card processing system.

9.3.3/2025.02.26
9.3.3/2025.02.26 is the current version of the Splunk credit card processing system, which has recently been identified with vulnerabilities.<SEP>9.3.3/2025.02.26 is the current version of Splunk, which has been identified as having recent vulnerabilities.<SEP>9.3.3/2025.02.26 is the current version of the system, released on February 26, 2025.

Production
Production is the live operational environment that underwent maintenance actions.

Uat
UAT refers to the user acceptance testing environment where maintenance actions were performed.

Fips Mode
FIPS mode is a security setting adjusted to 0 during the maintenance actions.

Pims
PIMS is a system that encountered a dbconenct issue during the upgrade process.

Dbconnect Issue
The dbconenct issue refers to a problem encountered with the PIMS search head during the upgrade process.

PA報表
PA報表is a report sent by Splunk, with its delivery status being checked during the maintenance process.<SEP>PA reports are monthly documents that sometimes encounter errors during rescheduling, requiring further investigation.

Cim_modactions索引
Cim_modactions索引is an index where Splunk reports are recorded and tracked.

DEP02
DEP02 is a system or service that was transferred and confirmed to be functioning correctly after maintenance.<SEP>DEP02 is a mainframe that requires scheduled backup scripts for KGIBIDX04 and CDIBHMGT02.<SEP>DEP02 is a server or device that will be transferred and has its root service confirmed.<SEP>DEP02 is a server or device that requires IP transfer and root service confirmation.<SEP>DEP02 is a device or system scheduled to be transferred next week.

IT部門
IT部門is the department responsible for confirming the receipt of Splunk reports via Exchange.

FISAC
FISAC is a system or service for which API IP requests are being submitted by B同事.<SEP>FISAC is an entity that requires API IP usage approval from James for data accommodation.<SEP>FISAC is the recipient of the IP application from James for API usage.<SEP>FISAC is an entity involved in API IP application requests.

SH KVstore引擎
SH KVstore引擎is an engine undergoing anomaly testing in a personal test environment.

LDAP設定
LDAP設定refers to the configuration of LDAP authentication for Splunk, completed by B部門.

Management搜尋異常
Management搜尋異常is an outstanding task related to search anomalies in management systems.

BTDC2問題處理
BTDC2問題處理is an outstanding task involving the resolution of issues related to BTDC2.<SEP>BTDC2問題處理involves handling issues related to BTDC2.

DEP02 root帳號使用確認
DEP02 root帳號使用確認involves confirming the use of the DEP02 root account, listed as an outstanding task.

Splunk Indexer Queue Full
The Splunk Indexer queue full is a technical issue that occurred on 20251211, requiring maintenance action.

G客戶
G客戶is the customer for whom maintenance services are being provided.<SEP>G客戶is the customer for whom maintenance services are being provided.

15.5%

2GB

記憶體使用率

0.31GB

CPU使用率

硬碟使用率
硬碟使用率denotes the hard disk usage rate, which was 43.73% and considered normal.<SEP>硬碟使用率is 42.91%, indicating normal operation within acceptable limits.

9.4.3 (2025.06.05)
9.4.3 is the current version of the system, released on June 5, 2025.<SEP>9.4.3 (2025.06.05) is the current version of Splunk, released on June 5, 2025.

10.0.2 (2025.11.14)
10.0.2 is the latest version of the system, released on November 14, 2025.

SOAR
SOAR is a security orchestration, automation, and response platform where SASE login success records are sent.

XSOAR
XSOAR is a platform that requires the addition of an index and HEC token for integration purposes.

Certificate
The Certificate refers to the digital credentials used for authentication and security purposes within Splunk.

Maintenance Service
The Maintenance Service involves replacing certificates and ensuring normal operation of Splunk systems.

Audit Log
The audit log is a record of system activities that continues to accumulate due to specific settings.

Messages-20251214 File
The messages-20251214 file is a single log file that has reached 148GB in size.

Clean.sh Script
The clean.sh script is used to manage the retention period of estreamer data, originally set to 12 hours but adjusted to 6 hours.

Audit Data
Audit data refers to the information logged by the audit system, which was manually deleted to free up space.

Rsyslog Forwarding Settings
Rsyslog forwarding settings are configurations that direct data to estreamer, contributing to high disk usage.

Logrotate Schedule
The logrotate schedule is a monitoring and management method for log files, set to check every 2 hours and rotate files exceeding 1GB.

Splunk Indexer Queue Full-20251215
Splunk Indexer queue full is a problem that occurred on December 15, 2025.

Workaround
The workaround involves adjusting RF/SF to 1/1 and redirecting all data to a single IDX.

Bucket Replication
Bucket replication is identified as the cause of the Splunk Indexer queue issue.

Splunk Indexer Queue
The Splunk Indexer Queue is an artifact that experienced issues, specifically being full.

PM Maintenance
PM Maintenance refers to periodic preventive maintenance activities.<SEP>PM Maintenance refers to preventive maintenance, a scheduled service performed on December 26, 2025 from 15:00 to 16:00.

635.27GB

10.0.1/2025.09.25
10.0.1/2025.09.25 is the latest version of Splunk's software as of September 25, 2025.

Splunk Software
Splunk Software is the product that underwent maintenance and performance evaluation.

Geodb
Geodb is a database that was updated as part of the weekly maintenance tasks.<SEP>Geodb is a database that was updated as part of the weekly maintenance tasks.<SEP>Geodb is a database that was updated as part of the maintenance tasks.<SEP>Geodb is a database related to geographical information, which was updated as part of the maintenance work.

AbuseIPDB
AbuseIPDB is a database that can detect threat intelligence information and directly integrate with Splunk.

Search Head
The Search Head is a component within the Splunk environment where testing for AbuseIPDB integration was conducted.

VirusTotal
VirusTotal is an online service for analyzing files and URLs using antivirus engines and website scanners, and it is being compared with data over a year old.<SEP>VirusTotal is a tool used for comparing intelligence data, with plans to explore similar detection tools for additional verification.

Data
Data over a year old is being compared with VirusTotal for threat intelligence purposes.

2025/11/24 11:30 to 2025/11/24 12:00
2025/11/24 11:30 to 2025/11/24 12:00 is the time period during which maintenance activities were conducted.

375 GB
375 GB is the current authorized usage limit for Splunk, which is recommended to be expanded due to high average usage.

銀行處11月月維護
銀行處11月月維護refers to the monthly maintenance activities performed on Splunk at the bank's location in November.

1398GB
1398GB represents the average authorization usage of Splunk, indicating a need for increased capacity.

f0eaf55d-ba9f-4a28-9514-bc1bff379d6d
f0eaf55d-ba9f-4a28-9514-bc1bff379d6d is the unique record number assigned to this maintenance activity.

Websebse Log
The Websebse log experienced abnormal truncation due to newline characters within the raw data.

Props.conf
Props.conf is a configuration file that was attempted to be modified to adjust truncation rules for the Websebse log.

Nix Logon Success Syntax
The Nix logon success syntax was confirmed as part of the maintenance actions.

SASE VPN Kick Session Report
The SASE VPN kick session report is a document created during the maintenance process.

20251030
20251030 is the date when the maintenance tasks were performed.

Tcpdump
Tcpdump is a network packet analyzer used to capture and display packets transmitted over a network.

Datamodel Dnsamd
Datamodel DNSAMD is a data model used for risk scoring and alerting, with specific focus on DNS traffic.

Es Alerts
ES alerts are monitoring systems that detect unusual activities, such as internal IP attempts to connect to dynamic domains or devices connecting to rarely seen ports.

Forcepoint Endpoint Dlp
Forcepoint Endpoint DLP is a data loss prevention system that was added to the endpoint.

Checkpoint Logclm Uf
Checkpoint Logclm UF is a log collection module that experienced delays in data handling.

Mitre Attack Framework
The Mitre Attack Framework is a knowledge base of adversary tactics and techniques based on real-world observations.

PA Traffic Events
PA Traffic Events are a type of event that were reclassified and adjusted for better handling in Splunk.

Firewall Event
The Firewall Event refers to an issue with the import timing of firewall events, which was addressed during the maintenance.

PA Events
PA Events are categorized into traffic, threat, or system types and were adjusted for better handling in Splunk.

Splunk On-Site Service

The process of checking the status of the equipment.

Search Page
The page used for searching events, where the IP address 172.20.137.45 could not be found.

No update is recommended due to the absence of major vulnerabilities or bugs.<SEP>The current version of Splunk is 9.3.3, released on 2025.02.26, with a recommendation to update to the latest version 10.0.0, released on 2025.07.28.<SEP>The current version of the Splunk system is 9.3.8, released on 2025.11.14, with a recommendation to upgrade to version 10.0.2 due to security vulnerabilities.

data.entity_name: Linux主機使用者與使用者權限
data.content:
Linux主機使用者與使用者權限

Sap Important Tcode Execution Record
The SAP important TCode execution record is a log that tracks specific actions within the SAP system. The alert syntax for this record was adjusted to accurately identify users by comparing it with Cyberark logs.

F5vpn Specific User Connection Record
The F5VPN specific user connection record is a log that tracks VPN connections made by specific users. It was adjusted to separate records related to the Digital Technology Center into two distinct reports.

Cyberark Logs
Cyberark logs are used to verify the actual users executing SAP TCode actions, ensuring accurate alert notifications.

Digital Technology Center
The F5VPN specific user connection record was adjusted to separate records related to the Digital Technology Center into two distinct reports.
